Plyra Open-Source Infrastructure · v0.1 Architecture
plyra-memory
Persistent structured memory for agentic AI · All frameworks · All environments · Docker-native
v0.1 — All 6 frameworks · Chroma · FAISS · OpenAI Embed · Pinecone v0.2 — Guard compose · Weaviate · Qdrant · OTel v0.3 — Cohere · Datadog · Multi-agent swarm isolation
01 — Full System Architecture
Agent
Clients
LangGraphmemory_node
→
AutoGenMemoryHook
→
CrewAIMemoryTool
→
LangChainChatMemory
→
OpenAI SDKMemoryAdapter
→
Plain PythonMemory()
MemoryClient · transport auto-selected by PLYRA_ENV
Transport
In-Processlocal / DI
|
TCP + msgpackQA / staging
|
gRPC + protobufpre-prod / prod
→
Schema Validator (Pydantic v2) — every write gateno untyped blobs ever enter the memory store
validated MemoryOp objects → STM Server core
STM Server
Core
WorkingMemorysession-scoped · size-bounded · auto-flush → episodic on session end
EpisodicMemoryappend-only · time-indexed · immutable · summarization trigger at N tokens
SemanticMemoryfact store · confidence-scored · decay model · auto-promoted from episodic
Hybrid Retrieval Enginevector similarity + structured SQL filters + recency decay → score fusion → token-budget ranked context
Semantic Cachecosine sim · 30–73% LLM call reduction
Consolidation Engineepisodic → semantic at N-access threshold
StorageBackend ABC + VectorBackend ABC (pluggable)
Storage
SQLitelocal · WAL · default
||
Postgresstaging · prod
·
Chromalocal vectors
|
FAISSlocal · GPU
|
Pineconeprod vectors
·
StdoutExporteralways on
|
OtelExporterprod traces
02 — Deployment Strategy (Docker Compose — Free, Zero-Infra, Works Everywhere)
Local / DI
No Docker Needed
Memory runs in-process — same Python process as the agent. Storage: ~/.plyra/memory.db (SQLite WAL). Vectors: Chroma local directory. No daemon, no port, no compose.

Start: import plyra_memory. Done.
zero configzero latency
QA / Pre-prod
docker compose up
One command. Ships with the repo. Starts:
• plyra-memory server (TCP :7700)
• ChromaDB (HTTP :8000)
• Postgres (:5432)
• Dashboard (:8766)

Agents point to PLYRA_SERVER=tcp://localhost:7700
docker compose upone file
Production
K8s Sidecar
plyraai/memory-server Docker image as sidecar in agent pod. gRPC on localhost:7701. Postgres + Pinecone as backends.

Same Docker image used in compose and K8s — identical binary, different config. No SPOF: sidecar is pod-local.
grpc sidecarsame image
docker-compose.yml — QA / Staging
Full Stack, One File
# docker-compose.yml — ships in the plyra-memory repo
version: "3.9"
services:

# ── MEMORY SERVER ──────────────────────────
memory-server:
  image: plyraai/memory-server:latest
  ports: ["7700:7700", "8766:8766"]
  environment:
    PLYRA_ENV: staging
    STORE_URL: postgresql://plyra:plyra@postgres:5432/memory
    VECTORS_URL: http://chroma:8000
  depends_on: [postgres, chroma]
  healthcheck:
    test: ["CMD","plyra-memory","ping"]
    interval: 10s

# ── POSTGRES (structured store) ─────────────
postgres:
  image: postgres:16-alpine
  environment:
    POSTGRES_USER: plyra
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: plyra
    POSTGRES_DB: memory
  volumes: ["pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data"]

# ── CHROMADB (vector store) ──────────────────
chroma:
  image: chromadb/chroma:latest
  ports: ["8000:8000"]
  volumes: ["chromadata:/chroma/chroma"]

volumes:
  pgdata:
  chromadata:
docker-compose.override.yml — Pinecone (Prod-like)
Swap Vectors to Pinecone
# docker-compose.override.yml
# docker compose -f docker-compose.yml \
# -f docker-compose.override.yml up

services:
  memory-server:
    environment:
      PLYRA_ENV: production
      VECTORS_BACKEND: pinecone
      PINECONE_API_KEY: ${PINECONE_API_KEY}
      PINECONE_INDEX: plyra-memory
      EMBEDDER: openai
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      GRPC_PORT: 7701
    ports:
      - "7701:7701" # gRPC
      - "7700:7700" # TCP fallback
      - "8766:8766" # dashboard

# Chroma not needed in prod — remove it
  chroma:
    profiles: ["local-only"]
03 — All External Clients (What We Connect To)
Client / System Category Protocol v0.1 Config Purpose Ship
LangGraph Framework Adapter direct Python custom memory_node inject context before node · record episode after exit v0.1
AutoGen Framework Adapter direct Python pre/post message hooks shared team memory · per-agent working context v0.1
CrewAI Framework Adapter Tool interface MemoryTool registered to crew crew-shared episodic log + per-agent working mem v0.1
LangChain Framework Adapter BaseChatMemory drop-in for ConversationBufferMemory full LangChain ecosystem compatibility v0.1
OpenAI Agents SDK Framework Adapter function calling memory_read / memory_write tools native tool-use pattern for OAI agent loops v0.1
Plain Python Direct API Python import Memory() object · zero deps base class, all adapters extend this v0.1
ChromaDB Vector Store local file / HTTP chromadb Python client default local vector index · zero infra v0.1
FAISS Vector Store in-process C++ faiss-cpu or faiss-gpu fast local ANN · GPU acceleration option v0.1
Pinecone Vector Store HTTPS REST PINECONE_API_KEY env var managed prod vectors · serverless billing v0.1
sentence-transformers Embedder in-process Python all-MiniLM-L6-v2 default local embedding · zero API key · CPU/GPU v0.1
OpenAI Embeddings Embedder HTTPS REST OPENAI_API_KEY env var text-embedding-3-small · 1536-dim · high quality v0.1
SQLite Structured Store local file ~/.plyra/memory.db · WAL mode episodes, facts, sessions, metadata · zero infra v0.1
Postgres Structured Store TCP + pg wire STORE_URL env var · asyncpg driver staging and production structured store v0.1
StdoutExporter Observability stdout JSON always on · same as plyra-guard structured log every memory op · debug default v0.1
OpenTelemetry Observability gRPC / OTLP OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT distributed tracing across agent swarms v0.2
plyra-guard Policy Gate direct Python Memory(policy="guard_policy.yaml") validates every write · blocks state poisoning v0.2
04 — STM Server: How to Build It
Server Internals — What Runs Inside
plyra-memory serve
# plyra_memory/server/app.py

class MemoryServer:
  """Single server, three transport modes"""

  def __init__(self, config: MemoryConfig):
    # Three memory layers
    self.working = WorkingMemoryLayer(config)
    self.episodic = EpisodicMemoryLayer(config)
    self.semantic = SemanticMemoryLayer(config)

    # Storage + retrieval
    self.store = build_store(config.store_url)
    self.vectors = build_vectors(config.vectors_url)
    self.embedder = build_embedder(config.embedder)
    self.retrieval= HybridRetrievalEngine(self)
    self.cache = SemanticCache(config)
    self.validator= SchemaValidator() # Pydantic v2

  async def handle(self, op: MemoryOp) → MemoryResult:
    op = self.validator.validate(op) # always first
    return await self.router.dispatch(op)
Transport Layer — Auto-Selected by PLYRA_ENV
One Server Binary, Three Transports
# plyra_memory/server/transport.py

def build_transport(config: MemoryConfig):
  match config.env:

    case "local" | "di":
      # No server — embedded in caller process
      return InProcessTransport()

    case "staging" | "qa":
      # TCP server · msgpack wire · asyncio
      return TcpTransport(
        host=config.host,
        port=7700,
        codec=MsgpackCodec(),
      )

    case "preprod" | "production":
      # gRPC sidecar · protobuf · HTTP/2
      return GrpcTransport(
        port=7701,
        tls=config.tls_cert,
      )
Wire Protocol — All Transports
MemoryOp API — Identical Regardless of Transport
Operation HTTP Equivalent gRPC Method Payload Returns
working.add(entry) POST /working WorkingAdd WorkingEntry(content, session_id, metadata) WorkingEntryID
working.get(session) GET /working/{session_id} WorkingGet session_id: str List[WorkingEntry]
episodic.record(event) POST /episodes EpisodicRecord Episode(event, tool, result, session_id, ts) EpisodeID
semantic.learn(fact) PUT /facts SemanticLearn Fact(subject, predicate, object, confidence) FactID · upserted
recall(query) POST /recall Recall RecallRequest(query, layers, top_k, filters) List[RankedMemory]
context_for(query) POST /context ContextFor ContextRequest(query, token_budget, weights) str · prompt-ready
session.flush(id) DELETE /working/{id} SessionFlush session_id: str EpisodeIDs written
health.ping() GET /health Ping — pong · uptime · version
05 — Framework Adapter Pattern (All 6 · v0.1)
LangGraph Adapter
custom memory_node — not a generic wrap
# plyra_memory/adapters/langgraph.py

from plyra_memory import Memory
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

def memory_node(memory: Memory):
  async def _node(state: dict):
    # inject context before LLM node runs
    ctx = await memory.context_for(
      state["messages"][-1].content,
      token_budget=1024
    )
    return {"memory_context": ctx}

  return _node

# Usage
memory = Memory()
graph.add_node("memory", memory_node(memory))
AutoGen Adapter
pre/post message hooks on ConversableAgent
# plyra_memory/adapters/autogen.py

class MemoryHook:
  def __init__(self, memory: Memory):
    self.memory = memory

  async def on_message_received(self, msg):
    # inject memory before agent processes
    ctx = await self.memory.context_for(msg.content)
    msg.context = ctx
    return msg

  async def on_message_sent(self, msg, response):
    # record episode after agent responds
    await self.memory.episodic.record(
      event="agent_response",
      input=msg.content,
      output=response.content
    )
CrewAI Adapter
MemoryTool — registered to crew agents
# plyra_memory/adapters/crewai.py

from crewai_tools import BaseTool

class MemoryTool(BaseTool):
  name: str = "plyra_memory"
  description: str = "Read and write agent memory"

  def _run(self, query: str) → str:
    return self.memory.context_for(query)

# Usage
agent = Agent(
  role="Researcher",
  tools=[MemoryTool(memory=shared_memory)]
)
LangChain + OpenAI SDK Adapters
Drop-in compatibility
# LangChain — extends BaseChatMemory
from plyra_memory.adapters.langchain import PlyraMemory

chain = ConversationChain(
  memory=PlyraMemory() # drop-in
)

# OpenAI Agents SDK — tool-based pattern
from plyra_memory.adapters.openai_agents import memory_tools

agent = Agent(
  name="my-agent",
  tools=[
    memory_tools.memory_read,
    memory_tools.memory_write
  ]
)
06 — Environment × Component Decision Matrix
Component Local DI / Dev QA Pre-prod Production
STM Mode In-Process In-Process TCP :7700 TCP / gRPC gRPC Sidecar :7701
Deployment pip install pip install docker compose up docker compose up K8s sidecar
Structured Store SQLite WAL SQLite WAL Postgres (Docker) Postgres (managed) Postgres (pooled)
Vector Store Chroma local Chroma local Chroma HTTP (Docker) Chroma / Pinecone Pinecone
Embedder sentence-transformers sentence-transformers OpenAI (optional) OpenAI OpenAI
FAISS optional (GPU) optional — — —
Semantic Cache in-memory dict in-memory dict in-memory in-memory in-proc per sidecar
Observability Stdout JSON Stdout JSON Stdout + Otel Otel traces Otel + Datadog
Dashboard localhost:8766 localhost:8766 docker :8766 hosted hosted + auth
plyra-guard compose off optional on on enforced
Config Source zero / defaults plyra_memory.yaml .env + yaml .env + yaml K8s ConfigMap + Secrets
PLYRA_ENV value local di staging preprod production
07 — PLYRA_ENV: One Variable Drives Everything
Config Resolution
No code changes between environments
# plyra_memory/config.py

class MemoryConfig(BaseSettings):
  env: str = "local" # PLYRA_ENV

  # auto-resolved from PLYRA_ENV
  store_url: str = "~/.plyra/memory.db"
  vectors_url: str = "chroma-local"
  embedder: str = "sentence-transformers"
  server_url: str | None = None

  model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
    env_prefix="PLYRA_",
    env_file=".env"
  )

# .env.local
PLYRA_ENV=local

# .env.staging
PLYRA_ENV=staging
PLYRA_SERVER_URL=tcp://memory-server:7700
PLYRA_STORE_URL=postgresql://plyra:plyra@postgres/memory
Client Auto-Connect
Agent code never changes
# This is THE ONLY CODE an agent ever writes.
# Works local, DI, QA, preprod, prod.

from plyra_memory import Memory

memory = Memory()
# ↑ reads PLYRA_ENV, auto-selects transport,
# auto-selects backends, connects or embeds.
# Identical across all 5 environments.

# working memory
await memory.working.add("user is debugging pipeline")

# episodic log
await memory.episodic.record(
  event="tool_call", tool="web_search", result="ok"
)

# semantic fact
await memory.semantic.learn("user prefers Python")

# hybrid retrieval → prompt-ready context
ctx = await memory.context_for(
  "help debug my LangGraph pipeline",
  token_budget=2048
)
plyra-memory · v0.1 Architecture · Apache 2.0
plyraai.github.io · @plyraAI · oss@plyra.dev
